diagrams.title: Centralized vs Decentralized Network Architecture
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing the structural difference between centralized and decentralized networks
elements: Centralized Network: Single central server/authority at center with all other nodes connected to it, single point of failure highlighted
Decentralized Network: Multiple distributed nodes interconnected peer-to-peer, no central authority, data replicated across all nodes
Arrows showing data flow: In centralized - all data flows through center; In decentralized - data flows directly between nodes
Legend identifying: Nodes, Central Authority, Data Flow Paths, Points of Failure
diagrams.purpose: Students will understand the fundamental architectural difference between centralized and decentralized systems, recognize single point of failure issues in centralized systems, and appreciate how blockchain eliminates central authority dependency. This is essential for university exam questions comparing centralized vs decentralized systems.

diagrams.title: Full Ecosystem Decentralization Architecture
diagrams.description: Layered architecture diagram showing three levels of ecosystem decentralization
elements: Layer 1 - Infrastructure Decentralization: Distributed servers, P2P network, node distribution, consensus mechanisms
Layer 2 - Application Decentralization: DApps, Smart Contracts, Decentralized Storage (IPFS), Decentralized Identity
Layer 3 - Governance Decentralization: DAO structure, Token-based voting, On-chain governance, Community decision making
Horizontal arrows showing inter-layer dependencies and data flow
Bottom label: Blockchain Protocol as the foundation connecting all layers
diagrams.purpose: Students will visualize how full ecosystem decentralization operates across multiple layers. This helps them understand that decentralization is not just technical but also applies to applications and governance. Essential for answering questions on components of full ecosystem decentralization.

diagrams.title: Blockchain Decentralization Process Flow
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing how transaction decentralization works in blockchain
elements: User initiates transaction → Digital signature using private key → Transaction broadcast to P2P network → Nodes validate transaction using consensus mechanism → Validated transaction added to block → Block added to blockchain → Distributed ledger updated on all nodes → Transaction confirmed
Decision diamond showing: If validation fails, transaction rejected
Icons for: Wallet, Network, Validator Nodes, Blockchain, Ledger
Arrow labels showing: Signed transaction, Broadcast, Validation, Block creation, Distribution
diagrams.purpose: Students will understand the step-by-step process of how decentralization is achieved in blockchain through consensus. This process flow is crucial for university exam questions on how blockchain achieves decentralization without central authority.

diagrams.title: Spectrum of Decentralization Levels
diagrams.description: Linear spectrum diagram showing degrees of decentralization from fully centralized to fully decentralized
elements: Left end: Fully Centralized - Single entity controls all aspects (traditional banks, government systems)
Intermediate 1: Centralized with distributed components - Main authority with distributed database
Intermediate 2: Federated/Consortium - Multiple authorities share control (consortium blockchain)
Intermediate 3: Majority decentralized - Core functions decentralized, some centralized services
Right end: Fully Decentralized - No central authority, all participants equal (public blockchain like Bitcoin, Ethereum)
Scale markers with percentages: 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%
Examples listed below each level
diagrams.purpose: Students will understand that decentralization exists on a spectrum and different systems can have varying degrees of decentralization. This helps them classify blockchain platforms and answer exam questions on types of decentralization.

diagrams.title: Decentralization Methods Classification
diagrams.description: Hierarchical diagram showing different methods of achieving decentralization
elements: Main branch: Methods of Decentralization
Sub-branches:
1. Technical Methods: Consensus algorithms (PoW, PoS, DPoS), P2P networking, Cryptographic hashing, Distributed storage
2. Economic Methods: Token economics, Incentive mechanisms, Token holders governance, Revenue distribution
3. Legal/Political Methods: DAO governance, On-chain voting, Reputation systems, Community consensus
4. Architectural Methods: Node distribution, Multi-signature schemes, Sharding, Layer 2 solutions
Each method connected to examples of blockchains using them
diagrams.purpose: Students will categorize and understand the various methods used to achieve decentralization. This comprehensive view helps in answering university exam questions on methods of decentralization and selecting appropriate methods for different applications.

charts.title: Centralized vs Decentralized Systems Comparison
charts.content: Two-column table with rows: Control (Centralized - Single authority; Decentralized - Distributed), Decision Making (Centralized - Top-down; Decentralized - Consensus-based), Data Storage (Centralized - Single database; Decentralized - Distributed ledger), Security (Centralized - Single point of failure; Decentralized - No single point), Speed (Centralized - Faster; Decentralized - Slower due to consensus), Scalability (Centralized - High; Decentralized - Limited), Trust (Centralized - Requires trust in authority; Decentralized - Trustless via code), Examples (Centralized - Banks, Google; Decentralized - Bitcoin, Ethereum)

charts.title: Benefits and Challenges of Full Ecosystem Decentralization
charts.content: Two-column flowchart showing benefits (Security through distributed validation, Transparency via public ledger, Autonomy through peer-to-peer, Censorship resistance, Innovation via open protocols) and challenges (Scalability limitations, Energy consumption, User experience complexity, Regulatory uncertainty, Governance difficulties, Network effects barrier). Each benefit/challenge connected to specific examples.

charts.title: Smart Contract Role in Decentralized Ecosystem
charts.content: Classification showing: Smart Contracts as: 1) Execution Layer (Auto-execute agreements when conditions met), 2) Trust Layer (Replace intermediaries), 3) Governance Layer (DAO voting mechanisms), 4) Value Transfer Layer (Token transfers, DeFi). Each role with example smart contracts and how they contribute to decentralization.

charts.title: Types of Decentralized Organizations
charts.content: Four-column comparison table: DAO (Decentralized Autonomous Organizations) - Member voting, Open participation, Transparent rules; DAC (Decentralized Autonomous Corporations) - Profit-driven, Token holders as shareholders, Automated revenue distribution; DAS (Decentralized Autonomous Societies) - Community governance, Social contracts, Collective decision making; DApps (Decentralized Applications) - Frontend + Smart contracts, No central backend, Token incentives. Columns: Definition, Governance, Economic Model, Examples.

branches: Definition: Distribution of control across network nodes, no single authority
Benefits: Security (no single point of failure), Transparency (public ledger), Autonomy (no intermediaries), Censorship resistance
Challenges: Scalability, Energy consumption, Regulatory issues, UX complexity, Governance
Methods: Technical (consensus, P2P), Economic (tokenomics), Legal (DAO), Architectural (nodes)
Full Ecosystem Layers: Infrastructure, Applications, Governance
Smart Contracts: Auto-execution, Trustless, Replace intermediaries
Applications: DeFi, Supply Chain, Identity, Voting, NFT platforms
Future: Layer 2, Cross-chain, Interoperability, Mass adoption
mindMaps.purpose: Comprehensive overview connecting all concepts from blockchain decentralization to full ecosystem decentralization. This mind map helps students integrate knowledge across all subtopics and prepare for exam questions requiring holistic understanding. Students can use this for quick revision covering definitions, benefits, challenges, methods, layers, and applications.

branches: Technical Foundation: Blockchain, Consensus, P2P Network, Cryptography
Governance Models: DAO, On-chain voting, Token governance, Community
Economic Layer: Tokenomics, Staking, Rewards, Defi
Application Layer: DApps, Smart Contracts, DeFi, NFTs
Infrastructure: Nodes, Distributed Storage, Oracles, Indexers
mindMaps.purpose: This concept map shows the interconnected nature of all components in a decentralized ecosystem. It helps students understand how different elements work together to create a fully decentralized system. Particularly useful for exam questions on how various components of blockchain technology contribute to decentralization and for understanding system design in blockchain applications.
